member.name: Rebecca Smith
member.synopsis: Rebecca Smith is the Conservative MP for South West Devon , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Opposition Assistant Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Private Sector
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 20 May 2026 to Question 934 on Parking: Fines, how many audits of parking companies have been undertaken by (a) DVLA and (b) the Government Internal Audit Agency since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency’s (DVLA) audit framework ensures that customers, including private parking companies, have appropriate internal controls in place to protect the data they request. Evidence based transactional checks are also conduc...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Anti-social Behaviour
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 3 July 2026 to Question 13039 on Aviation: Antisocial Behaviour, how many flights to or from UK airports have been diverted as a result of disruptive or unruly passenger behaviour in e
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on flights diverted due to disruptive or unruly passenger behaviour is reported to the Civil Aviation Authority through the Mandatory Occurrence Reporting scheme. Reports submitted under this scheme are subject to strict confidentiality requirements,...

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking Offences: Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 8 June 2026, to Question 4343, on Parking Offences: Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole, whether the higher parking charges from the trial remain in force in that locality.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The higher-level Penalty Charge Notice levels applied only during the August 2025 trial carried out by Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole Council.

writtenQuestions.heading: National Highways: Public Relations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 30 June 2026, to Question 13259, on National Highways: Expenditure, who are the stakeholders in relation to the external public affairs contract, and whether they included (a) Governme
writtenQuestions.answerText: The contract supports National Highways’ commitment to engage openly, effectively and appropriately with a wide range stakeholders who have an interest in the Strategic Road Network. This includes, but is not limited to, regional representatives, relevant...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether any strategic road schemes have been subject to funding reprofiling since 11 June 2026; and if she will list each scheme affected, the reason for the reprofiling, and the revised annual expenditure prof
writtenQuestions.answerText: There has been no funding reprofiling to enhancement schemes on the strategic road network since 11 June 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: A66: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether the A66 Northern Trans-Pennine project has been subject to any funding reprofiling since 11 June 2026; and what the planned expenditure on the project is in each financial year from 2026-27 to 2029-30.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The A66 Northern Trans-Pennine project is being delivered in line with the funding position set out following the Spending Review and the subsequent Road Investment Strategy.As with all major projects subject to Government approvals, the Full Business Cas...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aerospace Industry: BTEC Qualifications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that students who have studied BTEC Level 3 in Aerospace Engineering and similar qualifications are not prevented from accessing employer-led levy funded en
writtenQuestions.answerText: A learner is able to undertake an apprenticeship at a lower level and in the same sector as training they have previously received, providing the apprenticeship offers sufficient new learning to meet our minimum duration requirements. The government will ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Engineering
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment Skills England has made of the aviation sector’s reliance on overseas-based licensed engineers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Occupations related to aviation engineering such as Electrical and electronics technicians and Engineering technicians are included in the Migration Advisory Committee’s Temporary Shortage List review. As part of this review, government departments have b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Engineering
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether Skills England has undertaken an assessment of current and projected workforce shortages in aviation engineering, including the implications of new apprentice registrations remaining below pre-p
writtenQuestions.answerText: Skills England published an updated assessment of current demand for occupations in 2025. This is available here: https://explore-education-statistics.service.gov.uk/find-statistics/occupations-in-demand/2025. The assessment, based on a range of indicator...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hammersmith Bridge: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much funding has been provided by her Department towards Hammersmith Bridge since its closure in April 2019; and how much further funding has been requested by the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has provided the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham with £17m since the closure of Hammersmith Bridge in 2019. We would expect the Borough to request any further project funding via the Structures Fund, where it will be considered alo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bridges: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many local authority-owned highway bridges are subject to weight restrictions or closures because the estimated repair costs exceed the funding currently available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local highway authorities have a duty under Section 41 of the Highways Act 1980 to maintain the highways network in their area. My Department does not collect data on the reason for every weight restriction or closure applied to a bridge by a local highwa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Belfast and Dublin
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of a high-speed railway between Belfast and Dublin.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The railway in Northern Ireland is a fully devolved matter, including assessment of any new rail projects and proposals. The Northern Ireland Executive is responsible for their Infrastructure Strategy, including cross border cooperation with the Republic ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Traffic Control: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many people have (a) applied for, (b) commenced and (c) successfully completed air traffic controller training in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport and the Civil Aviation Authority do not hold data on the number of individuals who applied for, started, or successfully completed air traffic controller training in each of the last five years. Since 2022-23, the Civil Aviati...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Public Relations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has undertaken any review of whether expenditure by its arm's length bodies on external affairs and public affairs complies with Cabinet Office guidance on lobbying using public funds sin
writtenQuestions.answerText: Accounting Officers of DfT public bodies are bound by Managing Public Money and Cabinet Office guidance. Section 4.1 of the Code of Conduct for Board Members of Public Bodies also states, 'it will always be an improper use of public funds for public bodie...

writtenQuestions.heading: Vans: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what information her Department holds on the number and proportion of light commercial vans that, under normal operating conditions, would require (a) no en route charging, (b) one charging stop, (c) two chargi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold data on how many charging stops a particular number or proportion of light commercial vans (LCV) would require during a typical working day.The charging requirements of a van depend on a wide range of factors, including vehicl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what her Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing (a) procurement and (b) local content requirements for publicly supported vehicle purchases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting British businesses, including vehicle producers. All public procurement and Government subsidy must be compatible with our obligations under international agreements and UK Government Procurement Guidance. All pro...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Batteries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing additional tariffs on battery electric vehicles manufactured in China.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK continues to remain concerned about overcapacity and underlying market distorting practices, in particular the impact this could have on UK domestic industries such as battery electric vehicles. We continue to carefully assess options for addressin...

writtenQuestions.heading: Minibuses: Driving
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the relationship between the age of commercial minibus drivers and collision risk; and whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of changing the minim
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment of the relationship between the age of commercial minibus drivers and collision risk. The Driver Licensing Call for Evidence published in 2023 considered a range of driver licensing issues. The summary of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Shipping: Crew
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of the exemptions in the regulations governing the employment of young seafarers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Regulations relating to seafarer employment are developed in consultation with social partners including the maritime trade unions and shipowner representatives. They may be subject to post-implementation review.

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many veterans with amputations or other permanent injuries were reassessed for disability-related benefits in each of the last three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information could only be provided at disproportionate cost.
